punt_reason: Section block is 4178 words against target_max 700 (5.97× over). Subtraction profile: sentence_complexity -10 (40 sentences >35 words; rubric floor capped). Categories A/B/C/D/E individually do NOT reduce length when applied to a single <details> block — they restructure prose, not eliminate content. A Category A sentence-split pass over all 40 long sentences could raise understandability from 0 toward ~7 (sentence_complexity floor at -10 means there's also a deeper saturation issue worth investigating), giving composite ~4.2 (delta +4.2 clears 1.5 gate mechanically). But: (a) length remains 0 regardless because content volume is unchanged, so the surface stays below acceptable_floor 7.5 after one rewrite; (b) the underlying remediation for 4178-word sections is editorial — split the single <details id='p4-41-0'> into 4-6 sub-sections at natural argument boundaries, then per-block scoring brings each sub-section into bound. That's an analyst/operator content-architecture decision, not a Phase 2 mechanical rewrite. Per Phase 2 prompt's 'lightest-touch fix' rule, a single-pass Category A rewrite that leaves length=0 is not the lightest fix — it's the only fix Phase 2 has, but the categorical fix doesn't address the dominant defect (length).
category_gap_signal: section_details_block surfaces that are >3× target_max have a length problem that Categories A/B/C/D/E cannot resolve. Consider: (1) a new Category F = 'split section_details_block into multiple <details> blocks' (operator-led, but rewriter could propose split boundaries); (2) elevated section_details_block target_max for sections legitimately spanning multi-part arguments; (3) batch-deferral until operator-led section restructure lands.
recommendation_to_operator: Editorial split: this 4178-word block spans 4.1 Dashboard intro + sigma calculation + Fisher-combined audit. Natural split points at 'one ruler, more rooms' → separate <details> for (a) ruler definition + audit conventions, (b) sigma derivation, (c) more-rooms expansion. Once split into ~1000-word sub-blocks, each is rewrite-eligible within Category A scope at sloppytoppy-score's next pass.
